title: Fwd: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)
preview:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 7:15 AM
Subject: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch pla...
text:
From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, Feb 13, 2026 at 7:15 AM
Subject: RRB-17: rollback loop in private installer (root cause + patch plan)

Root cause (RRB-17): when CRD migration partially applies, the rollback cleanup hook exits non-zero before clearing `installer-rollback-lock`. The controller treats rollback as failed and schedules another rollback attempt, which sees the lock and repeats. Net effect: "stuck rollback" / infinite rollback retries.

Patch plan:
- In rollback cleanup: clear lock in a `defer`-style best-effort step even if migration revert fails.
- Add lock TTL (annotation with timestamp) and refuse to retry rollback if lock older than N minutes; surface actionable status.
- Add metric `ins...

Date: Tue, 10 Feb 2026 15:22:00 -0500
Subject: DPA redline + runtime carveout request

Hi Rishi — thanks again for the call today. As discussed, Legal at Helios attached a clean redline of our DPA with two asks that are blocking procurement: 1) a carveout for ephemeral runtime telemetry used solely for model quality debugging (retention <= 7 days), and 2) an explicit rollback/rollback-notice clause if a subprocessor change introduces a data handling change.

We're flexible on language but need Product & Security to confirm it won't prevent your roadmap (esp. dedicated/private customers), or whether we need an exception path. Attaching our redline.

Sarah
Sr. Contracts Manager, Helios Health

---

Subject: ClearGlass SLO qualification and action brief

Hi Samir / Tanya,

Thanks again for the call earlier — quick recap and proposed next steps so the team has a single reference. We focused on the streaming use case for invoice extraction (avg sequence ~1.2k tokens) and the customer-facing chat assistant. Key SLO targets you asked us to qualify against:

- P95 latency: 250ms per response for single-turn chat (max 2k tokens)
- Tail latency requirement for streaming (first token <= 120ms, steady-state <= 40ms per chunk)
- Sustained throughput: 150 reqs/sec, with burst to 400 reqs/sec for 2-3 min windows
- Concurrency: 500 concurrent users (avg 1 request every 10s)

Subject: SeedCloud / Redwood — discovery follow-up

Thanks for the call today — really helpful to walk through the options. Quick recap from our side and a couple items for you: 

Pain points we called out:
- Tail latency spikes impacting interactive search (current p95 450-600ms on our 256-token tests)
- Bursty traffic around business-hours peaks (short, high-concurrency bursts ~300–400 req/s)
- Cost sensitivity on per-token billing for large models; want to avoid overprovisioning
- Model size constraints: ML team prefers to keep a single 70B-ish model if possible rather than many distillations

Goals for a POC:
- Demonstrate consistent p95 latency under 150ms for 256-token responses
- Verify streaming reliability and prefix reuse (customers often repeat prompts)

Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, Aug 12, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Lumen Legaltech — Terraform support for Dedicated autoscaling bounds (min/max + predictive)

Vanessa — looping you in on a customer requirement that’s now gating Lumen’s Dedicated rollout.

Lumen Legaltech (enterprise) will only manage Dedicated config via IaC (Terraform). They specifically need the new scaling policy fields:
- min_replicas / max_replicas (hard bounds)
- predictive_mode toggle (on/off) + any required fields

They’re asking for:
1) When Terraform support will be available (even beta is fine)
2) Any interim workaround (API/Console is a non-starter for change management)

Do we have an ETA / PR in flight? Lauren, FYI in case this impacts their timeline.

Thanks,
Irene

Jordan —

Our platform (OrbitAI) experienced significant latency spikes and partial availability loss on June 16 between 02:18 and 06:41 UTC. We saw a sharp increase in failed inference calls and a 35–50% latency increase for critical endpoints. This impacted our production customers and triggered our contractual SLA review.

Per section 3.2 of our contract, we are requesting confirmation of eligibility for service credits and a preliminary quantification of the expected credit. Attached are our client-side logs and a short timeline (orbitai_service_logs_0616.txt). Please let us know what information you need from our side to move this forward.

Hi Elin,

text:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>
Cc: NordBank Procurement <procurement@nordbank.example>
Date: Sun, Feb 16, 2025 at 9:14 AM
Subject: Re: NordBank - data residency (EU only) and failover clarification

Hi Elin,

Confirming what we discussed: NordBank traffic will be served in EU (eu-west) under normal operations. We can support a *time-bounded exception* to allow failover serving in the US region if the EU region is degraded or during scheduled DR test windows.

Key point: we have payload logging disabled for your tenant, so we do not persist prompts/responses as logs. During failover, operational metrics (request ids, timings, error rates) may be processed in the failover region.

We’re drafting a short exception memo for your procurement file.

Thanks,
Camila

—
From: Elin Sjöberg <elin.sjoberg@nordbank.example>

Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, Feb 12, 2025 at 9:18 AM PT
Subject: Route experiments guardrails: soft-stop vs hard-stop (decision)

Team — we need to lock the behavior for guardrails in beta/GA so Console copy + backend semantics match.

Question: when a guardrail triggers (e.g., p99 latency regression, error rate spike, cost/token blowup), do we automatically stop the experiment (“hard-stop”), or do we mark as degraded + notify and require operator action (“soft-stop”)?

Proposal (recommended):
1) Initial release (beta + GA): SOFT-STOP only
- System evaluates guardrails continuously and sets experiment.status = DEGRADED when triggered
- Co...
